samples.xml_description:
Design an unsigned multiplier with 8-bit inputs and 16-bit output using pipelined processing. Clock signal for timing. Active-low reset for initializing the module. Enable signal for controlling the multiplication operation. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Output enable signal reflecting the state of mul_en_in. Output product. Number of pipeline stages, value is 8. Register for output enable signal. Register for storing the multiplicand. Register for storing the multiplier. Register for accumulating partial sums. Register for output product.

The multi_pipe_8bit module implements an unsigned multiplier with 8-bit inputs and a 16-bit output using a pipelined architecture. It begins with input control, where the module monitors the enable signal mul_en_in to control the multiplication operation. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the module captures the value of mul_en_in and stores it in the register mul_en_out_reg. The output enable signal, mul_en_out, is derived from the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg.

The module has two input registers, mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg, used to store the multiplicand and multiplier, respectively. These registers are updated on the positive edge of the clock signal, but only when the input enable signal is active. The module generates partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. The partial products are stored in a set of wires, temp, with each wire representing a different bit position.

The module calculates the partial sums by performing addition operations on the partial products. It uses a set of registers, sum, to store the intermediate sum values. Each register corresponds to a group of partial products and is updated on the positive edge of the clock signal. The final product is calculated by summing up all the partial sums. The result is stored in the register mul_out_reg, which is also updated on the positive edge of the clock signal. Finally, the module assigns the output product value to mul_out, reflecting the value in mul_out_reg, or outputs 0 if the output enable signal is inactive.
